name: New York-Style Cheesecake
recipeIngredient: 1/2 cup graham cracker crumbs (4 double crackers)
5 packages fat-free cream cheese (8 oz. pkgs., softened)
recipeInstructions:
1. Preheat oven to 350°F. Grease an 8-inch springform pan and sprinkle the bottom with cracker crumbs.
2. In a large bowl, mix together sugar and cornstarch. Add softened cream cheese and beat with an electric mixer on medium speed until smooth. Beat in eggs and vanilla until blended. Reduce speed to low and add heavy cream, beating until smooth. Pour mixture into prepared pan and spread evenly.
3. Bake for 1 hour or until a skewer inserted near the center comes out clean; the center of the cake should still be jiggly. Allow cooling on a wire rack (the cake will set completely as it cools). Cover loosely and refrigerate overnight or up to 3 days.
4. About 1 hour before serving, run a knife around the edges of the cake and carefully remove the pan sides. Arrange peaches in a border around the top of the cake and fill the center with blueberries.
5. Melt apricot preserves in a saucepan over low heat or in the microwave. Strain through a fine mesh sieve. Brush over the fruit on top of the cheesecake.
